channel.title: Data Science on Renato Vassallo
channel.description: Recent content in Data Science on Renato Vassallo

item.title: Structural macroeconomic forecasting using a text-based measure of policy uncertainty
item.description: This study employs a text-based uncertainty indicator within a Bayesian VAR framework to conduct robust structural macroeconomic forecasting. Our analysis utilizes monthly US data on Economic Policy Uncertainty (EPU), Industrial Production, Consumption, and Employment spanning the period from 1985 to 2019. Subsequently, we construct structural forecasts by assuming a predetermined trajectory for EPU, starting from March 2023. To account for the inherent uncertainty surrounding the model parameters, we propose a Bayesian estimation method employing independent Normal-Wishart priors.

item.title: Sentiment Analysis for Monetary Policy Predictions
item.description: This project highlights the scope for using text data from central bank statements to improve predictions about policy rates. The problem is constructed as a classification into three potential outcomes: the rate in the next period goes up, goes down, or stays the same. We test our approach with data from the central banks of Australia, Chile and Peru. Main findings We find that a Random Forest Classifier trained only on economic data (e.

item.title: Spatial interpolation of apartment prices using Airbnb web scraped dataset
item.description: The purpose of this article is to to compare the results of several interpolation methods for spatial data of Airbnb apartment prices in Barcelona. Applying text mining techniques, an Airbnb web scraped dataset from 2022 is developed, and different interpolation methods are applied, including Voronoi approach, Nearest Neighbors (NN), Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW), and ensemble methods. Main findings Based on the RMSE value, the NN interpolation method with n = 5 yields much more accurate prediction values than the Voronoi or IDW methods.

item.title: Tracking Twitter Conversations on the COVID-19 Pandemic
item.description: The objective of this Technical Note is to understand how the conversation on Twitter about COVID-19 evolved during the beginning of the pandemic. In particular, we are interested in tracking only Canadian medical doctor users. To do this, the first step was to collect a large-scale dataset of COVID-19-specific geotagged tweets using the Twarc library, then filter and clean the tweets to obtain some descriptive statistics that could serve as input for further research.
